developer.comment: Founded in 2017 by Andrew Mason (Groupon co-founder).

first-release.comment: Descript launched publicly in December 2017; month-year precision from launch coverage.

status.comment: Actively developed; current pricing and feature pages live as of June 2026.

license.comment: Proprietary product with a genuinely usable free tier (60 media min/mo), so freemium per the tool's own commercial model.

primary-type.comment: Core job is transcription paired with media-by-text audio/video editing; not positioned as a real-time dictation or meeting-bot assistant.

real-time-dictation.comment: Descript transcribes recordings/uploads; it is not a speak-to-type dictation input method.

file-transcription.comment: Core feature: import/upload audio or video files and transcribe them.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting bot that joins calls live; the Zoom integration imports cloud recordings after the meeting, which is file transcription, not live capture.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Batch-transcribes finished recordings/uploads rather than producing a running live transcript.

audio-sources.comment: Records from microphone and accepts file uploads; can also import from cloud sources (Zoom/Google Drive), but those are imported files rather than live system/meeting audio.

underlying-engine.comment: Descript uses ASR and has historically evaluated/used third-party engines (e.g. Google), but does not clearly disclose a single current production engine, so left null per RESEARCH.md (do not infer).

language-count.comment: Approx; Descript transcription supports ~22-26 Latin-alphabet languages per its docs/reviews. Overdub (voice synthesis) is English-only.

english-accuracy.comment: No Descript-specific independent WER found; reviews consistently rate its automatic English transcription as high-quality but still needing light correction. Qualitative 4/5, not a published WER.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Transcription language is selected in app settings rather than auto-detected; no documented automatic language detection.

custom-vocabulary.comment: No documented user-facing custom dictionary / vocabulary-boost mechanism; corrections are made in the editor after transcription.

speaker-diarization.comment: Automatic speaker detection; Speaker Detective plays a clip of each detected speaker to help name them.

timestamps.comment: Word/segment timing underpins media-by-text editing and SRT/VTT subtitle export.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Underlord AI assistant produces summaries, clip selection, social posts and script drafts from the transcript.

built-in-editor.comment: Hallmark media-by-text editor: edit the audio/video by editing the transcript words.

translation.comment: Translation with proofreading (30+ languages) listed on the Business plan.

export-formats.comment: Transcripts export as plain text, Word (DOCX), PDF and Markdown; subtitles as SRT/VTT. No documented JSON transcript export.

processing-location.comment: Transcription and AI processing run in Descript's cloud; no fully local on-device transcription path.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud-based transcription and AI features require a network connection.

data-used-for-training.comment: Production AI models use no Descript user data; sharing transcription data to improve the service is disabled by default and only enabled by the user, i.e. opt-in.

data-deletion-control.comment: Deleting a project permanently removes it from Descript servers within 30 days.

platforms.comment: Native desktop apps for macOS and Windows plus a Chromium-based web app; no native iOS or Android app as of June 2026.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; no OS-wide speak-to-type into other apps.

integrations.comment: Native Zoom (cloud-recording) import and Google Drive/Adobe/Slack-bot integrations; only Zoom maps to a defined integration tag here. Broader connectivity via Zapier.

api-automation.comment: Descript API is available in beta, with an MCP server and Zapier automation for power users.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier plus paid monthly/annual subscription tiers (Hobbyist/Creator/Business/Enterprise).

free-tier.comment: Free plan with 60 media minutes/month, not just a time-limited trial.

free-allowance.value: 60 media min/mo, 100 one-time AI credits, 720p watermarked export
free-allowance.comment: Free plan limits as of June 2026.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan (Hobbyist) is $16/user/mo billed annually; $24/mo if billed monthly. Checked June 2026.
